mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized demand and supply weakness, which forces equity risk premiums higher and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive R-squared by at least 0.02 over a baseline single-proxy model, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising inflation expectations outpace nominal rate hikes, compressing real yields and forcing equity valuations to contract, which yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes, or the spread between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no statistically significant correlation with equity valuation multiples over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases, which translates into immediate earnings momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the 5-day forward return distribution shows no mean reversion or momentum effect relative to the GDP surprise.

mechanism_narrative: A breach of the industrial production index below 105 signals capacity utilization stress that disrupts supply chains and elevates input costs, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO breaches the 105 threshold, or the threshold crossing fails to predict earnings revision downgrades in a walk-forward backtest over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and rising unemployment create a wage-price spiral risk that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform a random walk benchmark in out-of-sample prediction, or forward 60-day returns are not significantly lower when both series deteriorate concurrently compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, accelerating housing starts reflect robust consumer credit conditions that sustain discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of late-cycle housing acceleration episodes, or HOUST growth shows no statistical relationship with consumer discretionary sector returns over a 10-year sample.
